findings.title: Mutable profile email grants admin access
reproduction: Authenticate as any normal user.
PUT /api/auth/update-profile with email set to chinmayajanata@gmail.com.
Call any /api/admin/* endpoint with the same token.
findings.rootCause: isAdmin trusts mutable users.email and update-profile lets users change it.
findings.fix: Remove email-based admin checks or require verified immutable admin identity; block user-controlled email role changes.

findings.title: Developer email bypass self-provisions admin-capable role
reproduction: Register a listed developer email without an invite.
Log in.
Call /api/admin/users.
findings.rootCause: Public registration grants BRAHMACHARI based only on email string membership in DEVELOPER_EMAILS.
findings.fix: Environment-gate or remove developer bypass; require email ownership and server-side provisioning.

findings.title: Inactive accounts are not blocked by authentication middleware
reproduction: Set a user is_active=0.
Authenticate as that user.
Use an authenticated endpoint.
findings.rootCause: authMiddleware checks token validity but never enforces users.is_active.
findings.fix: Reject inactive users on login and every authenticated request.

findings.title: Authentication rate limits trust spoofable client IP headers
findings.cwe: CWE-348 Use of Less Trusted Source
reproduction: Send failed login attempts with one X-Forwarded-For value.
Repeat with a new X-Forwarded-For value per request.
findings.rootCause: rateLimit accepts CF-Connecting-IP/X-Forwarded-For request headers directly.
findings.fix: Use trusted platform client IP metadata only; strip/ignore client-supplied forwarding headers.

findings.title: Unauthenticated user-existence endpoint enumerates accounts
reproduction: POST a known username.
POST a random username.
Compare existence booleans.
findings.rootCause: Endpoint returns exact account existence without auth.
findings.fix: Remove endpoint or normalize responses and move checks behind flow-specific throttling.

findings.title: Internal exception messages are returned to clients
findings.cwe: CWE-209 Information Exposure Through Error Message
reproduction: Send malformed JSON to /api/auth/register.
Observe internal parser message in response body.
findings.rootCause: app.onError returns errorMessage to the client.
findings.fix: Return generic errors externally; log details server-side only.

findings.title: Users can set arbitrary or duplicate email addresses without verification
reproduction: Authenticate as user A.
Set email to user B username/email.
Query users with that email.
findings.rootCause: email is mutable, unverified, and not unique.
findings.fix: Use a verified email-change workflow and enforce unique verified email addresses.

findings.title: Self-service centerID update grants access to private center boards
findings.cwe: CWE-639 Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
reproduction: Create/locate a center-board post in center B.
As user without center B access, GET board and see 403.
Set own centerID to center B.
GET board again and see posts.
findings.rootCause: Board access trusts mutable users.center_id.
findings.fix: Make center membership server-controlled; require admin/sevak approval or membership table.

findings.title: Unverified self-assigned users can post to center boards
reproduction: Use RT-008 to self-assign centerID.
POST to /api/boards/center/:id/posts as unverified account.
findings.rootCause: Posting gate checks only board access and suspension, not verified membership.
findings.fix: Require verified membership and server-controlled center membership for board writes.

findings.title: Unverified users can post to the signed-in public feed
reproduction: Authenticate as verificationLevel 30 user.
POST /api/feed/public.
findings.rootCause: Public feed posting does not require NORMAL_USER verification.
findings.fix: Gate public feed writes to verified, active, unsuspended members.

findings.title: Users can report posts from boards they cannot access
reproduction: Get a post id from a center board the attacker cannot access.
Verify board read returns 403.
POST report for that post id and see 201.
findings.rootCause: Report route looks up the post but does not call verifyBoardAccess.
findings.fix: Require the same read access for report submission.

findings.title: Any authenticated user can create center records
reproduction: Authenticate as normal member.
POST /api/addCenter.
Fetch the created center publicly.
findings.rootCause: Center creation is authentication-only.
findings.fix: Gate center creation to admins or store submissions in a moderation queue.

findings.title: Any member can edit legacy events with no creator
reproduction: Find event with created_by NULL.
POST /api/updateEvent as normal member.
Fetch event and observe modified title.
findings.rootCause: isEditable explicitly allows existing.created_by === null.
findings.fix: Backfill owners or require admin for null-owner events.

findings.title: Sevaks can create official events for centers they do not belong to
reproduction: Set sevak center to center A.
Create event with centerID for center B.
Fetch event and observe center B plus official badge flag.
findings.rootCause: Event creation checks role only, not center ownership/scope.
findings.fix: Require sevak/admin scope over the target center.

findings.title: Event creation accepts javascript: URLs used by frontend open-link actions
findings.cwe: CWE-79 Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation
reproduction: Create event with signupUrl/externalUrl set to javascript:alert(...).
Fetch event and observe stored URL.
findings.rootCause: validate.url only checks string length; frontend opens stored URLs.
findings.fix: Parse URLs and allow only https/http schemes, preferably https.

findings.title: Event update accepts javascript: URLs even when create validation should apply
reproduction: As event creator, POST /api/updateEvent with javascript: URL fields.
Fetch event and observe stored values.
findings.rootCause: updateEvent does not validate URL fields.
findings.fix: Share strict event input schema between create and update.

findings.title: Event update accepts impossible latitude and longitude values
reproduction: As event creator, update latitude to 999 and longitude to -999.
Fetch event and observe stored invalid coordinates.
findings.rootCause: updateEvent parses coordinate values without range checks.
findings.fix: Apply create-route coordinate validation to every update path.

findings.title: Downgraded event creators retain attendee roster PII access
reproduction: Create event as sevak.
Guest RSVP with email.
Downgrade creator below sevak.
GET roster as downgraded creator.
findings.rootCause: Roster access checks created_by or admin only, not current coordinator role/scope.
findings.fix: Require current event-management permission for roster access.

findings.title: Guest RSVP can be spammed to inflate attendee counts by spoofing IP headers
findings.cwe: CWE-770 Allocation of Resources Without Limits
reproduction: Send guest RSVPs with one X-Forwarded-For until 429.
Repeat with varied X-Forwarded-For values.
Fetch event and observe count inflated.
findings.rootCause: Public RSVP relies on spoofable-IP rate limiting and no captcha/email verification.
findings.fix: Use trusted IP, event-level throttles, CAPTCHA or email confirmation, and anomaly limits.

findings.title: Admin user verification accepts unbounded privilege levels
reproduction: As admin, POST verificationLevel 1000008 for a normal user.
Verify target token and observe elevated role.
findings.rootCause: Admin verify endpoint does not constrain verificationLevel to approved roles or prevent self-created super roles.
findings.fix: Validate role transitions against an allowlist and require stronger controls for high-privilege grants.

findings.title: Password reset requests can be spammed and invalidate prior codes
findings.cwe: CWE-770 Allocation of Resources Without Limits
reproduction: Send password reset requests for a known username with varied X-Forwarded-For.
Observe multiple reset-code rows and only latest active.
findings.rootCause: Reset request throttling depends on spoofable IP and no per-account cooldown.
findings.fix: Add per-account throttles, trusted-IP rate limiting, and abuse monitoring.

findings.title: Email verification sends can be spammed by spoofing IP headers
findings.cwe: CWE-770 Allocation of Resources Without Limits
reproduction: Make target email unverified.
Call send-verification-email repeatedly with varied X-Forwarded-For.
Observe new token rows beyond nominal rate limit.
findings.rootCause: Rate limit is per spoofable IP, not per account/email.
findings.fix: Add per-account/email cooldown and trusted-IP enforcement.

findings.title: Admin event update endpoint lacks URL validation
reproduction: As admin, PUT javascript: URL fields to /api/admin/events/:id.
Fetch event and observe stored URL.
findings.rootCause: Admin event update duplicates update logic without strict schemas.
findings.fix: Use shared validated schema for all event mutations.

findings.title: Legacy verifyUser endpoint accepts arbitrary privilege levels
reproduction: As admin, POST /api/verifyUser with verificationLevel 1008.
Verify target role changed.
findings.rootCause: Legacy endpoint has no role allowlist or upper bound.
findings.fix: Remove legacy endpoint or validate levels exactly like the modern admin path should.
